title: throw; vs throw ex; — сохранение стека

cards.prompt: throw; preserves the original stack trace, so the originating method name Origin still appears in ex.StackTrace; Contains(Origin) is True
cards.code: using System; string r; void Origin(){ throw new InvalidOperationException("x"); } void Middle(){ try { Origin(); } catch (Exception) { throw; } } try { Middle(); } catch (Exception e) { r = e.StackTrace!.Contains("Origin").ToString(); } Console.WriteLine(r);

cards.prompt: throw e; updates (resets) the StackTrace property to the rethrow line, so the originating method Origin is lost; Contains(Origin) is False
cards.code: using System; string r; void Origin(){ throw new InvalidOperationException("x"); } void Middle(){ try { Origin(); } catch (Exception e) { throw e; } } try { Middle(); } catch (Exception e) { r = e.StackTrace!.Contains("Origin").ToString(); } Console.WriteLine(r);

cards.prompt: Same origin rethrown two ways: throw; keeps the Origin frame (True), throw e; loses it (False); the deterministic marker Contains(Origin) distinguishes them
cards.code: using System; string Test(bool useThrowSemicolon){ void Origin(){ throw new InvalidOperationException("x"); } try { try { Origin(); } catch (Exception e) { if (useThrowSemicolon) throw; else throw e; } } catch (Exception e) { return e.StackTrace!.Contains("Origin").ToString(); } return "n/a"; } Console.WriteLine($"rethrow={Test(true)} throwEx={Test(false)}");
